name: Garlic Chicken- Szechuan
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Sliced bamboo shoots or cooked carrots
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop chicken breasts into 2-inch square pieces.
2. Mix marinade ingredients (egg white, salt, sugar, sesame oil, and dry sherry) in a medium bowl.
3. Add chicken pieces to the marinade and mix well; let stand at least 20 minutes.
4. Heat 1 cup of oil in a wok over medium heat for 1 minute.
5. Add chicken pieces to the hot oil and stir-fry until nearly cooked, about 3-4 minutes. Remove chicken, draining excess oil over the wok. Set aside.
6. Remove most of the oil from the wok, leaving 4 tablespoons.
7. Dissolve cornstarch in chicken stock to make a paste; set aside.
8. Heat remaining oil in the wok over medium heat for 1 minute.
9. Add cooked chicken, garlic, water chestnuts, and bamboo shoots (or carrots) to the wok. Stir-fry about 2 minutes.
10. Add salt, sugar, soy sauce, and water; cook over low heat for 10 minutes.
11. Add cornstarch paste; stir-fry until sauce thickens slightly, about 30 seconds.
12. Stir in 1 teaspoon of sesame oil. Serve hot. Makes 4 servings.
